evidence_quotes.quote: All of these practices above violates EU Directive 93/13/EEC.

paragraphs.text: Dear Members of the European Parliament and Commission. I won't lie, Stop Killing Games and the possibility that payment processors like Visa and Mastercard censorship cartel behavior are among the things that brought me here. I've come to respectfully request the things below. 1st. i would love to see this act also include protection on digital content and products like video games, audiobooks, music, etc. That we the consumers already purchased. From being prevented from retaining, and from being permanently disabled by publishers and companies. 2nd. To prevent payment processor companies (Visa, Mastercard, etc). From controlling to what we can and can't buy. And from denying to process payment on content, products, and works that aren't considered illegal. As their irrational censorship could spread to content and products that isn't even explicit, and could in the long run could stagnate multiple industries. And destroy creativity, the freedom of expression and free speech. They should not get to dictate on what legal content we get to consume. All of these practices above violates EU Directive 93/13/EEC. And Visa has been caught lying as shown in the attached file. Don't be surprised if other payment processors decides to lie aswell.
